revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Hida-jinja]][[ja:斐太神社]] {{Expand Japanese|斐太神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Hida Shrine | native_name = 斐太神社 | image = Hida-jinja (Myoko), haiden.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Hida Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|37.053708|138.235692}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi|ca|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|es|Ōkuninushi|fa|اوکونینوشی|fr|Ōkuninushi|hu|Ókuninusi|id|Ookuninushi|it|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|ko|오오쿠니누시|mni|ꯑꯣꯀꯨꯅꯤꯅꯨꯁꯤ|pl|Ōkuninushi|pt|Ookuninushi|ru|Окунинуси|simple|Ōkuninushi|sr|Okuninuši|sv|Ōkuninushi|tr|Ōkuninushi|uk|Окунінуші|vi|Ōkuninushi|zh|大國主|qid=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} }} {{nihongo|'''Hida Shrine'''|斐太神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Echigo Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Echigo Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Echigo Province|ja|越後国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11636502|1=List of Shikinaisha in Echigo Province}} ** Series ordinal: 13 ** P155: {{ill|Q135040436|ltq=Q135040436|qid=Q135040436|1=Matotano Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135040438|ltq=Q135040438|qid=Q135040438|1=Miyakeno Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Myōkō, Niigata|lt=Myōkō|ar|ميوكو (نييغاتا)|azb|میوکو، نیقاتا|ceb|Myoko-shi|ce|Мёко (Ниигата)|de|Myōkō (Stadt)|en|Myōkō, Niigata|envoyage|Myoko|es|Myōkō (Niigata)|fa|میوکو، نیگاتا|fi|Myōkō|fr|Myōkō|gl|Myōkō|id|Myōkō, Niigata|it|Myōkō|ja|妙高市|javoyage|妙高市|ko|묘코시|ms|Myōkō, Niigata|mzn|میوکو، نیگاتا|nl|Myoko|pl|Myōkō (miasto)|pt|Myōkō (Niigata)|ro|Myōkō|ru|Мёко (Ниигата)|sv|Myōkō|tg|Мёко|tl|Myoko, Niigata|tr|Myōkō (şehir)|tt|Мёко (Ниигата)|uk|Мьоко (Ніїґата)|uz|Myōkō (Niigata)|vi|Myōkō, Niigata|war|Myōkō, Niigata|zh_classical|妙高市|zh_min_nan|Myoko-chhī|zh_yue|妙高|zh|妙高市|qid=Q279642|1=Myōkō, Niigata}} * {{ill|Q666514|ltq=Q666514|qid=Q666514|1=Echigo Province}} * {{ill|Q11664982|ltq=Q11664982|qid=Q11664982|1=Kubiki district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|37.053708|138.235692}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == image (P18) == * Hida-jinja (Myoko), haiden.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q276944|ltq=Q276944|qid=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 182431 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|ltq=Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == legal form (P1454) == * {{ill|religious corporation|lt=religious corporation|ja|宗教法人|qid=Q7311343|1=religious corporation}}https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * ひだじんじゃ == postal code (P281) == * 944-0097https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == TripAdvisor ID (P3134) == * 17379925 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4110005009872 == Commons category (P373) == * Hida-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '新潟県妙高市宮内字屋敷連241', 'language': 'ja'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Hida-jinja (Myoko), torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Hida-jinja}} [[JA:斐太神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11500718}} == Japanese content == {{特殊文字}} {{神社 |名称 = Hida Shrine |画像 = [[File:Hida-jinja (Myoko), haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|qid=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Myōkō|Q279642|qid=Q279642|1=Myōkō, Niigata}}Miyauchi, Aza Yashiki-zure 241 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|37|03|12.8|N|138|14|08.5|E|display=ti}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Grand-shrine-style {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} (kirizuma-zukuri), tile roof |別名 = Yashiro-san |札所等 = |例祭 = May 3-4 (spring festival), Nov 3 (autumn festival) |神事 = March 3 (fire-quelling festival) | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=Hida Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Hida Shrine''' (ひだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Miyauchi, {{ill|lt=Myōkō|Q279642|qid=Q279642|1=Myōkō, Niigata}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|qid=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It stands facing east on a hill west of the Miyauchi settlement, on the eastern foot of a branch ridge extending from Aota Minamihayama. Around the shrine are the {{ill|lt=Hida ruins group|Q85766470|qid=Q85766470|lt=Hida ruins|1=Hida Sites}}; to the north are the {{ill|lt=Kannondaira-Tenjindō Kofun Group|Q17990293|qid=Q17990293|lt=Kannondaira kofun group|1=Kannondaira-Tenjindō Kofun Group}}, and to the south the {{ill|lt=Kannondaira-Tenjindō Kofun Group|Q17990293|qid=Q17990293|lt=Tenjindō kofun group|1=Kannondaira-Tenjindō Kofun Group}} (designated a national {{ill|lt=Historic Site|Q1081138|qid=Q1081138|1=Historic site}} together as "Kannondaira-Tenjindō Kofun Group"). === Enshrined deities === With {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} (Yachihoko-no-Kami) as principal deity, it also enshrines {{ill|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (Tsumihane Yae Kotoshironushi-no-Kami) and {{ill|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}} (Suwa Ōkami) as {{ill|lt=ai-dono|Q135009883|qid=Q135009883|1=Aidono}} deities. These two were moved from other shrines: Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto, also called Yashiro Ōkami/Yashiro Myōjin, is said to have stood until {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1574|Q6680|qid=Q6680|1=1574}}) at Okazawayama upstream of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yashiro River|1=UNKNOWN}}; because this deity is enshrined here, the shrine is popularly called "Yashiro-san." Takeminakata-no-Mikoto is said to have stood on the summit of Aota Minamihayama. In the past {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Kōtaijin|1=Amaterasu}} was also enshrined. An alternative theory in ''Echigo Kubiki District Gazette Draft'' (1901) introduces another deity theory, Arahito-no-Mikoto. In ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|qid=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}''''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', section Right Capital, Imperial descent, upper., Arahito is described as the fifth-generation descendant of Kose Okarano Sukune, son of {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|qid=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}, and ancestor of Kose Hita no Ason and Kose Hita no Omi. Maruyama Motosumi's ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hokuetsu Fudoki Setsuge|1=UNKNOWN}}'' (1686) also lists this as the deity. There is also a theory making Kose Okarano Sukune-no-Mikoto the deity (''{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824|qid=Q11588824|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō Studies}}'', ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Echigo Yashi|1=UNKNOWN}}''), and because Hata no Yashiro no Sukune was Okarano Sukune's elder brother, some argue the co-enshrined Yashiro Myōjin may be Hata no Yashiro no Sukune-no-Mikoto{{ill|lt=Kurita Hiroshi|Q6536170|qid=Q6536170|1=Kurita Hiroshi}}, ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shingi Shiryo|1=UNKNOWN}}'' (compiled 1873).. === History === [[File:Hida Site, Yashiroyama-B, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Hida ruins (national historic site)}}]] As noted above, present Hida Shrine is a merger of Hida deity (Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto), Yashiro Myōjin, and Suwa Daimyōjin. Of these, the original Hida Shrine (shikinaisha) is said first to have stood on the mountain. On the right bank of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uchikawa (Niigata)|1=UNKNOWN}}, a tributary of Yashiro River, at Tobita Aza Yashikizoe in Myōkō, there was said to be a satomiya with a haiden. The old site is thought possibly to be Miyazawa north of the Samegao Castle ruins west of the shrine''Shikinaisha Survey Report''., but the time and reason for relocation to the current site are unknown. One theory says it was originally called "Hita Myōjin" and changed to "Hida Myōjin" in the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|qid=Q1374325|lt=Engi era|1=Engi (era)}} (early 10th century)''Place Names of Niigata Prefecture''.. To the west and north of the present site lie pit-dwelling groups of Uenotaira and Hyakuryōyama (part of the Hida ruins) interpreted as building remains; further north lies Kannondaira Kofun Group, and to the south Yashiroyama pit-dwelling group (also in Hida ruins) and Tenjindō Kofun Group. It has been suggested that the power that built Kannondaira and Tenjindō kofun may have worshipped Hida Shrine. Shrine tradition, however, says Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto came to this region with his child deities Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto and Takeminakata-no-Mikoto, stayed here, taught rice cultivation, and managed the land; at that time he named the area "Hidakami no Kuni" in {{ill|lt=Koshi Province|Q1351611|qid=Q1351611|1=Koshi Province (Japan)}}, from which the place name "Hida" aroseHistorically, "Hida" referred collectively to present Miyauchi, Yukimori, Tobita (in Myōkō) and Inari (in Jōetsu), and there is also a place name "Takami" in Tobita (''Shikinaisha Survey Report'').. In this account, Ōkuninushi and Takeminakata developed mountains and fields, while Kotoshironushi (Yashiro Myōjin) controlled marshes and rivers and opened waterways. According to ''{{ill|lt=Daidō Ruijuhō|Q11433347|qid=Q11433347|1=Daidō Ruijuhō}}'' of {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|qid=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=808|Q30366|qid=Q30366|1=AD 808}}), this shrine had a special medicine for stroke called "Samizu-yaku," whose formula had been transmitted by {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}}; it was also a hereditary medicine of the Ōgami clan, junior district officials of Kubiki District. Later, under the Engi system, Hida deity was listed as a small shrine in Kubiki District, {{ill|lt=Echigo Province|Q666514|qid=Q666514|1=Echigo Province}}; Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto stood as protector of riverbanks deep in Okazawayama upstream of Yashiro River (leaving the river name), and Takeminakata-no-Mikoto stood on Aota Minamihayama summit. Though the date is unknown, they are said to have been merged into Hida Shrine, and traces remain on the summit. In the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period|Q204023|qid=Q204023|1=Sengoku period}}, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|lt=Uesugi clan|Q1150058|qid=Q1150058|1=Uesugi clan}}. When Samegao Castle was built on the mountain west of the shrine, Hida Shrine was designated the {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|qid=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}} for 128 nearby villages, and shrine lands were donated from villages including Miyauchi, Jingūji, Otsuyoshi, Kagochō, Yukimori, Aota, Inari, Tōkaichi, and TobitaExcept Inari, which belongs to Jōetsu City, all are in Myōkō City.. In Tenshō 2, {{ill|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080|qid=Q311080|1=Uesugi Kenshin}} is said to have merged Yashiro Myōjin (a stone shrine remains at the former site), revering the three deities as protectors of the castle's {{ill|lt=kimon|Q11673539|qid=Q11673539|1=Kimon}} direction. The shrine burned in warfare at Samegao Castle's fall in Tenshō 7 ({{ill|lt=1579|Q6709|qid=Q6709|1=1579}}), and burned again in {{ill|lt=Meireki|Q1192766|qid=Q1192766|1=Meireki}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1656|Q6982|qid=Q6982|1=1656}}); documents burned with the buildings, so detailed history is unclear. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, in {{ill|lt=Tenna|Q1145289|qid=Q1145289|lt=Tenna|1=Tenna}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1683|Q7651|qid=Q7651|1=1683}}) land survey, the shrine received 5 koku 3 gō of tax-exempt land. After the {{ill|lt=Sakakibara clan|Q2975351|qid=Q2975351|1=Sakakibara clan}} entered as lords of {{ill|lt=Takada Domain|Q839422|qid=Q839422|1=Takada Domain}} in Echigo, it received continued patronage. The adjacent Jingūji district south of Miyauchi is named for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Jingūji (Jōetsu)|1=UNKNOWN}} temple (now in Teramachi, Jōetsu), formerly this shrine's {{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|qid=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}, said to have moved to Jōetsu in Edo times. In December {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}}) it was ranked village shrine; on Nov 3, Shōwa 17 ({{ill|lt=1942|Q18625|qid=Q18625|1=1942}}), promoted to gōsha. Promotion to prefectural shrine was planned for Nov 3, Shōwa 20 but cancelled by defeat in war. On May 11, Shōwa 33 ({{ill|lt=1958|Q5253|qid=Q5253|1=1958}}), fire completely destroyed the shrine buildings. === Precincts === The honden is 3-by-3 bay, gable-entry {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|qid=Q13308575|1=Kirizuma-style}}, called "grand-shrine-style Shinmei-zukuri" by the shrine. The haiden is 3-by-4 bay kirizuma-zukuri gable-entry. Both are tile-roofed and connected by a kirizuma-zukuri stone room passage. A {{ill|lt=magatama|Q334232|qid=Q334232|1=Magatama}} and {{ill|lt=sekitai|Q11586501|qid=Q11586501|1=sekitai}} said to have been excavated in the precinct are treasured but undesignated. File:Hida-jinja (Myoko), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Hida-jinja (Myoko), honden.jpg|Honden File:Hida-jinja (Myoko), torii.jpg|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Hida-jinja (Myoko), Karita-jinja.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Karita Shrine}}]] Karita Shrine ({{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} and {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}}), plus stone-hokora Inari and Hachiman shrines are enshrined. === Festivals === At the March 3 fire-quelling festival, "Harukoma dance" is performed at the {{ill|lt=naorai|Q11581721|qid=Q11581721|1=Naorai}} feast: a woman wearing a {{ill|lt=tanzen|Q9210345|qid=Q9210345|1=tanzen}} and holding a phallic symbol, and a man in women's {{ill|lt=juban|Q55358825|qid=Q55358825|1=Zhongyi}} holding a female symbol, red cloth, and bells, dance with faces covered. === Priests === The Kurashina family serves hereditarily. The family claims Fujiwara lineage and says that when {{ill|lt=Emperor Juntoku|Q357249|qid=Q357249|1=Emperor Juntoku}} was exiled to {{ill|lt=Sado Province|Q1063844|qid=Q1063844|1=Sado Province}}, they moved from Kyoto to Kurashina village in {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|qid=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}}, {{ill|lt=Sarashina District|Q7423207|qid=Q7423207|1=Sarashina District, Nagano}} (present Kurashina, {{ill|lt=Chikuma|Q179757|qid=Q179757|1=Chikuma, Nagano}}, Nagano) as communication liaison, settled there, later moved to Echigo, and became priests of this shrine. === Access === * About 35 minutes west on foot from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Myōkō Haneuma Line|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Kita-Arai Station|Q5355811|qid=Q5355811|1=Kita-Arai Station}} * {{ill|lt=Kubikino Bus|Q11265488|qid=Q11265488|1=Kubikino Bus}} route 48 Hida line{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/soshiki/kotsu/bus-jikoku.html|title=Comprehensive timetable of public transport in Jōetsu City|publisher=Jōetsu City|accessdate=2020-04-02}} to "Hida Kita Elementary School-mae," then about 8 minutes southwest on foot (weekdays only, few services) === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === {{参照方法|date=2020-03|section=1}} * ''Shikinaisha Survey Report'' Vol.17 Hokurikudō 3, Kogakkan University Press, 1987 * ''Place Names of Niigata Prefecture'' (Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 15), Heibonsha, 1986 ISBN 4-582-49015-8 === External links === {{Commonscat|Hida-jinja}} * [https://www.city.myoko.niigata.jp/docs/856.html Hida Shrine] - Myōkō City website {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Hida Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hida Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hida Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:妙高市の歴史]] [[Category:妙高市の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:新潟県の旧郷社]] [[Category:高田藩]]
